title: Unit, Property, Replay, Headless SDL, Render, and Playtest Evidence · Behavior Case: Render Contract
summary: Tests assert implementation details, use real time and global random state, compare one screenshot, never inject failures, and call dummy video proof of usability. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the render contract increment in risk-layered automated and human game test suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Test draw records, layers, transforms, asset bounds, and selected golden outputs with controlled tolerances and review.
Use dummy video and audio drivers for adapter integration while labeling missing window, GPU, timing, device, and sensory evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-render-contract — Test draw records, layers, transforms, asset bounds, and selected golden outputs with controlled tolerances and review.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-testing-headless-fixtures-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-testing-headless-fixtures-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_testing_headless_fixtures_5(unit_cases: int = 24, property_cases: int = 40, headless_cases: int = 9, changed_defect_detected: bool = True, dummy_driver_verified: bool = True, render_contract_checked: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if min(unit_cases, property_cases, headless_cases) < 1:
        return False, unit_cases, "test-layers"
    if not changed_defect_detected:
        return False, unit_cases, "mutation-survived"
    if not dummy_driver_verified:
        return False, headless_cases, "headless-gap"
    if not render_contract_checked:
        return False, headless_cases, "render-gap"
    total = unit_cases + property_cases + headless_cases
    assert total >= 3
    return True, total, "risk-layered-game-tests"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

options.text: Keep the Render Contract artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the render contract defect evidence boundary for Render Contract.

For repair the render contract defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Render Contract for risk-layered automated and human game test suite.
Reconstruct the conditions for Render Contract, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test draw records, layers, transforms, asset bounds, and selected golden outputs with controlled tolerances and review.
